mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns exceed 55% when rates rise.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where unemployment rises above the 0.5 percentage point threshold over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth outpaces the federal funds rate increase, consumer demand remains resilient despite monetary tightening, supporting corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when retail sales growth is below 2% annually while rates rise, or Rank IC drops below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which signals immediate economic momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production changes coincide with negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 50% of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher total nonfarm payrolls indicate robust employment growth and rising household income, which directly supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window or payroll growth shows no statistical correlation with 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening and erodes real corporate margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in greater than 65% of months where CPI exceeds the 0.4% monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in housing starts and industrial production signals synchronized strength across construction and manufacturing sectors, boosting capital expenditure cycles and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 55% of cases when housing starts decline while industrial production rises.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2% confirms sustained economic expansion and validates corporate earnings forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in greater than 60% of quarters where GDP growth surpasses 2% during early-cycle phases.
